title: Default
objective: Start the default workstream for Meeting Ingest.
status_summary: Session focused on validating and hardening the Meeting Ingest personal agent workflow. Ran meeting-ingest init, processed inbox files first with mock and then corrected workflow to provider=session. Fixed a real DOCX Teams speaker parsing bug for comma-form names and contractor labels, added extraction regression tests, and isolated a CLI scaffold test from repo-local config; verification stayed green with uv run pytest => 113 passed. Configured local Meeting Ingest project for session provider by default. Processed multiple real transcripts through the two-phase session flow, producing useful markdown artifacts and signal JSONL outputs. Added docs/session-provider-inbox-agent-workflow.md and updated AGENTS.md, README.md, and docs/implementation-plan.md so agents know that 'process the inbox' means session-provider processing, not mock/API. Created active Claude Code skill ~/.claude/skills/meeting-ingest, archived legacy ingest-meeting skill under docs/legacy/claude-ingest-meeting-skill, and replaced active legacy ingest-meeting SKILL.md with a deprecation shim. Added Claude Code sub-agent ~/.claude/agents/meeting-ingest-session-provider and durable repo source docs/claude-agents/meeting-ingest-session-provider.md. Created Codex skill ~/.codex/skills/meeting-ingest with durable repo source docs/codex-skills/meeting-ingest/SKILL.md. Validated Claude Code natural prompt now selects meeting-ingest and successfully processes inbox files through session provider. Current important product gap remains: ingest-inbox rejects provider=session, so agents manually run provider-request -> response JSON -> phase 2 per file; this should become a first-class session inbox workflow. Working tree has intentional uncommitted changes across docs, skills, tests, parser fix, local runtime project state, and iQ Context state.
next_actions: Create the roadmap implementation plan recommended after the product review. It should sequence V1 completion polish, output modes and repair/regenerate workflows, first-class session inbox automation, provider/wrapper hardening, stakeholder playbook V1, migration/existing corpus adoption, broader communication artifact ingest, and iQ Context integration. Separate ready-to-implement items from areas still needing design decisions, and include acceptance criteria for each roadmap layer.
